feature: MCP health check: block metadata and link-local addresses
description: Harden check_mcp_health to reject URLs resolving to link-local, metadata, multicast, and unspecified address ranges while allowing loopback and private ranges for local MCP servers
workflow_rationale: Single-service security fix in one function with complementary test coverage - no multi-phase dependencies or complex integration requirements

phases.name: Implementation & Testing
phases.description: Add URL host validation helper and harden check_mcp_health endpoint with comprehensive test coverage

subtasks.description: Create _is_safe_mcp_url helper function that resolves URL hosts and validates against blocked address ranges (link-local, metadata, multicast, unspecified) while allowing loopback and private ranges
Integrate _is_safe_mcp_url into check_mcp_health endpoint - reject unsafe URLs with status=unknown and logged reason (not returned to client)
Write comprehensive tests covering: 169.254.169.254 (metadata), IPv6 link-local literal (fe80::1), file:// scheme, http://localhost, http://127.0.0.1, private 10.x address, and public hostnames

acceptance_criteria: Unresolvable hosts fail closed (treated as unsafe)
Response maintains existing status: unknown format
Rejection reason is logged, not returned to client

verification_steps.name: Unit Tests
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All 6+ test cases pass (metadata, link-local IPv4, link-local IPv6, file scheme, loopback, private, public)

verification_steps.name: Manual Verification
verification_steps.command: Manual - verify check_mcp_health response shape unchanged
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Response contains serverId, status (unknown|healthy|unhealthy), and message

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk - security-critical SSRF guard change. Existing response shape must be preserved, blocking behavior must be fail-closed, and comprehensive test coverage is mandatory.
